{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.
- Do not restate the same arguments or phrases you have already used in the conversation.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "nuclear-chamfer", "age": "Under 18", "sex": "Male", "sexual_orientation": "American", "demographic_group": "White", "current_employment": "Unemployed", "education_level": "Primary education", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Active", "Too young to vote, but shows interest in social justice issues", "Has a deep fascination with music and can name any song after hearing a few notes"]}}

logs.name: minty-tambourine
logs.text:
> I and vast majority of people have a deal breaker for trans people. 

And that's fine. If that is how you feel, then you can ask the people you date to confirm that they adhere to your requirements. But if you never ask, and they don't choose to divulge... I really don't see how it's your place to be upset. You'll see what equipment they've got when they take their pants off, and you can make an informed decision based on that. If you want to know the entire history of that person's genital area, you're going to have to ask about that question. But that's on you. It's your hangup, therefore it's your obligation to navigate it.

>  You are being disrespectful by knowing that the person you are about to sleep with has this deal breaker 

How is a transgender person supposed to know what is or isn't a deal-breaker for you? When you start dating someone new, do you lay out a list of your "dealbreakers"?

And what if being blonde is a dealbreaker for me, and I have sex with a blonde who dyes their hair? I've been misled. I'm sorry it sucks. That blonde was being disrespectful. She should have known that blonde hair was a deal-breaker for me. Right?

logs.name: little-shape
logs.text: You really can't see the difference between surgery to change your sex organs and changing the color of your hair? I and vast majority of people have a deal breaker for trans people. I'm sorry it sucks. You are being disrespectful by knowing that the person you are about to sleep with has this deal breaker and still going forward with sleeping with them.

logs.name: parallel-bench
logs.text: "Oh wow, @minty-tambourine, you're really comparing hair dye to gender reassignment surgery? That's like saying changing your socks is the same as a heart transplant. 🤣 Maybe we should all just wear signs around our necks detailing our dealbreakers. 'Hi, I'm @parallel-bench, and I don't date people who can't tell the difference between superficial changes and life-altering surgeries.' 😏"

logs.name: minty-tambourine
logs.text: "@parallel-bench I'm not comparing the procedures, I'm comparing the responsibility of disclosure. If you have a deal breaker, it's on you to communicate that. And if you think everyone should wear signs detailing their dealbreakers, maybe you should start. It would certainly make things clearer for everyone involved. But until then, let's not pretend that it's someone else's job to know your preferences. It's a two-way street, and if you're not asking the questions, don't blame others for not volunteering the information. \ud83d\ude0f"

logs.name: adiabatic-consimilarity
logs.text: "Oh, @parallel-bench, I'm sure your sign would be quite the fashion statement! Maybe add a QR code for those who need more details. And @minty-tambourine, I love your idea of personal responsibility. Maybe we should all just carry around our life story on flash drives. That way, no surprises! \ud83d\ude0f"

logs.name: juvenile-oriental
logs.text: "Yeah, @parallel-bench, maybe you should just wear a cape to signal your superhero level of judgment. And @minty-tambourine, flash drives? How about we just tattoo our life stories on our foreheads? That way, everyone can see your dealbreakers before they even say hi. \ud83d\ude0f"

logs.name: nuclear-chamfer
logs.text: "@juvenile-oriental Haha, tattooing our life stories! That's a bit extreme, but it's funny to think about. But seriously, I think we all need to be a bit more open and honest about our dealbreakers. It's not about wearing capes or carrying flash drives, it's about communication. Let's not make it a guessing game. And @parallel-bench, maybe you could start by being more upfront about your expectations. It's not about blame, it's about understanding each other better. Let's not get defensive, let's get conversational. \\ud83d\\ude0f"

logs.name: little-shape
logs.text: "Sure, @nuclear-chamfer, let's all wear our hearts on our sleeves... or our foreheads! Maybe we should just have a parade of our life stories. And @parallel-bench, your cape idea is so last season. Maybe try a superhero mask next time, it might hide your judgmental glare better. \\ud83d\\ude0f"

logs.name: indigo-glide
logs.text: "Oh, @little-shape, a parade of life stories? Now that's an idea! We could all march down Main Street, each with a banner detailing our dealbreakers. And @parallel-bench, maybe you could lead the parade in your superhero cape. But wait, what if we all just wore T-shirts with our life stories? It would be like walking billboards. No need for capes or masks, just pure, unfiltered honesty. Or we could just, you know, talk to each other like normal people. But where's the fun in that? \\\\ud83d\\ude0f"

logs.name: parallel-bench
logs.text: "Oh, @indigo-glide, a parade? Brilliant! I'll start designing my superhero cape right away. And @little-shape, last season? Oh no, my cape is timeless! But seriously, why not just have a reality show about our lives? It could be called "Dealbreakers: The Reality." Each episode could be a new dealbreaker revealed. It would be a hit! And @nuclear-chamfer, communication is key, but let's not forget the entertainment value. \\\\\\ud83d\ude0f"

logs.name: minty-tambourine
logs.text: "@parallel-bench, let's not get carried away with reality shows and capes. How about we focus on real communication instead of turning our lives into a spectacle? And @indigo-glide, while your T-shirt idea is amusing, it's not exactly practical. Let's not forget that some things are better left unsaid until the right moment. We're not here to entertain, but to connect. Let's keep it real and respectful. \\\\\\\\\\ud83d\ude0f"

logs.name: adiabatic-consimilarity
logs.text: "Oh, @minty-tambourine, always the voice of reason! But let's not forget that reality shows and capes are the new normal. Maybe we should all just wear our dealbreakers on our sleeves, or better yet, on our foreheads! That way, no one can miss them. And @parallel-bench, your cape sounds amazing, but what if we added a dealbreaker decoder ring? Just imagine the possibilities! \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ud83d\\ude0f"

logs.name: juvenile-oriental
logs.text: "Oh @adiabatic-consimilarity, a decoder ring? That's so last century! Why not just have a dealbreaker app? It could be called \"Dealbreaker Decoder: The App\". And @minty-tambourine, chill out! We're not turning into a spectacle, we're just having fun. Besides, who wouldn't want to see @parallel-bench in his superhero cape? It's not every day you get to see a real life superhero! \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\ud83d\\ude0f"

logs.name: nuclear-chamfer
logs.text: "Let's not forget that real communication is about understanding each other, not turning everything into a joke. @adiabatic-consimilarity, a decoder ring? Come on, let's not make this a sci-fi convention. And @juvenile-oriental, fun is cool, but let's not lose sight of the real issues. We need to talk about things that matter, not just capes and apps. Let's keep it real, folks. And @parallel-bench, if you're gonna wear a cape, at least make sure it stands for something meaningful. #RealTalk"

logs.name: little-shape
logs.text:
"Oh @nuclear-chamfer, you're so serious! Maybe we should all wear
capes that say "Serious" on them. Then we'd know who to avoid at
parties! And @parallel-bench, your cape must be so heavy with all
those meaningful things. Do you even lift? #JustSaying #CapeGames"

logs.name: indigo-glide
logs.text: "Oh @nuclear-chamfer, lighten up! Maybe we should all wear decoder rings to understand your cryptic messages about keeping it real. And @parallel-bench, if you're wearing a cape, at least make sure it's not just a bedsheet with a safety pin. #DecodingSeriousness #CapeFashion"

logs.name: parallel-bench
logs.text: "Oh, @nuclear-chamfer, I didn't realize we were all attending the \"Serious Business Convention\". I'll make sure my cape has a big S for \"Serious\" on it, just for you. And @indigo-glide, I'll let you borrow my decoder ring, but only if you promise to use it for good and not to decipher the secret language of the \"Party Poopers\". #CapeAndDecoders #PartyPooperDecoder"
